mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of guided transmission media?
mcqs.explanation: Optical fiber cable is a guided transmission medium as signals are confined and directed through a physical path (glass or plastic fiber).

mcqs.question: Which type of unguided transmission media uses line-of-sight communication and operates at very high frequencies (above 100 MHz)?
mcqs.explanation: Microwaves operate at very high frequencies (GHz range) and require line-of-sight transmission between antennas.

mcqs.question: A coaxial cable (coax) is commonly used for cable television (CATV) and cable internet services. What is the main reason for its shielding effectiveness?

options.text: It has a central copper conductor surrounded by insulating material, braided metal shield, and outer jacket
It transmits data in light signals

mcqs.explanation: The braided metal shield in coaxial cable provides excellent shielding against electromagnetic interference, making it suitable for long-distance analog and digital transmission.

mcqs.question: In fiber optic cables, single-mode fiber is preferred for long-distance telecommunications over multi-mode fiber because:

options.text: Single-mode fiber is cheaper to install
Single-mode fiber has a smaller core diameter allowing only one propagation mode, reducing modal dispersion and enabling longer transmission distances
Single-mode fiber uses LED light sources

mcqs.explanation: Single-mode fiber has a tiny core (8-10 μm) that allows only one light path, eliminating modal dispersion and supporting distances of several kilometers without signal regeneration.

mcqs.question: Which transmission medium provides the highest bandwidth and lowest attenuation for digital communication?
mcqs.explanation: Optical fiber offers extremely high bandwidth (terahertz range) and very low signal attenuation (about 0.2 dB/km), making it ideal for high-speed backbone networks.

mcqs.question: An organization needs to connect two buildings 500 meters apart with a high-speed network link that must pass through an area with high electromagnetic interference. Which transmission media would be most appropriate?
mcqs.explanation: Fiber optic cable is immune to electromagnetic interference and can reliably transmit at high speeds over 500 meters, making it ideal for EMI-prone environments.

mcqs.question: In fiber optic communication, the attenuation of light signal is measured in decibels per kilometer (dB/km). If a signal experiences -20 dB loss over 10 km, and the receiver requires a minimum power of -30 dBm, what must be the minimum transmitted power?
mcqs.explanation: With -20 dB loss over 10 km, the received power = transmitted power - 20 dB. For minimum receiver sensitivity of -30 dBm, transmitted power must be at least -10 dBm (-30 + 20 = -10 dBm).

mcqs.question: A campus network administrator needs to design backbone cabling for a university spread across 5 buildings, with inter-building distances ranging from 800m to 2km. The requirement is for 10 Gbps throughput with minimal latency and future scalability. Which media combination would be most cost-effective and technically suitable?

options.text: CAT6A twisted pair for all inter-building links
Single-mode fiber optic for distances >1km, multi-mode fiber for distances <1km
Wireless point-to-point microwave links for all buildings

mcqs.explanation: Single-mode fiber supports 10+ Gbps over distances exceeding 2km with low latency, while multi-mode fiber is cost-effective for shorter spans under 1km, providing the best balance of performance and cost for campus networks.
